title: pinus teocote subsp. quinquefoliata
intro: Pinus teocote subsp. quinquefoliata, also known as the five-needled pinyon pine, is a subspecies of tree that belongs to the Pinaceae family, native to Mexico. Traditionally, the plant's needles and seeds have been used in herbal medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Pinus teocote subsp. quinquefoliata include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of the plant may include teas, infusions, and tinctures made from the needles or seeds, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
